rules.text:
Magic in Fabula Ultima falls within one of two categories: spells or Rituals. Spells represent codified magic formulas developed to protect warriors and travelers: their applications are primarily offensive or defensive. They can be learned easily and only require a brief gesture to cast, but lack flexibility. Rituals are complex magical effects designed to overcome a specific obstacle: whenever a Ritual is performed, it will always be started from scratch and take several minutes to complete. While powerful and extremely flexible, Rituals consume large amounts of Mind Points and require difficult Magic Checks; additionally, failing a Ritual will cause the magic to spiral out of control.

There are six magic disciplines: Arcanism, Chimerism, Elementalism, Entropism, Ritualism, and Spiritism. Five correspond to a Class (Arcanist, Chimerist, Elementalist, Entropist, Spiritist), whilst Ritualism is available to all of those Classes except Arcanists. Spells can belong to Chimerism, Elementalism, Entropism, and Spiritism. Arcanism and Ritualism do not feature any spells. Chimerist spells must be learned from creatures via the Spell Mimic Skill.

CASTING A SPELL: You must be free to move your body (you can hold items as long as you can move your arms), be able to speak and pronounce the invocation, and have enough Mind Points to pay the total MP cost (including any additional MP used to enhance the effect). Unless the target is Self or Special, you must be able to see your targets. To cast: (1) Declare the spell and its effect. (2) If required, choose targets. (3) Perform the gestures and pronounce the formula (nearby creatures will see and hear you). (4) Spend the required Mind Points. (5) If the spell is offensive, perform a Magic Check. Elementalism, Entropism, and Spiritism Checks rely on INS + WLP; Chimerism may choose between INS + WLP or MIG + WLP. Non-offensive spells are automatically successful and require no Check. Fumbles and critical successes on Magic Checks generate opportunities as normal. (6) Apply the effects.

MP COST: To cast a spell you must have enough MP to pay its cost; some spells allow you to spend more MP to increase the number of targets or strengthen the effect. A cost of 10 x T costs 10 Mind Points per target; the final amount required is the total MP cost.

TARGETS: A target of 'Self' means the caster uses the spell on themselves; 'Special' indicates the spell functions in a unique way described in its text.

DURATION: Either 'Instantaneous' (takes effect immediately then dissipates) or 'Scene' (creates an ongoing effect lasting until the end of the scene it was cast in).

CASTING FURTIVELY: It is impossible to cast a spell furtively; everyone nearby will immediately know that magic is being used, and who by.

OFFENSIVE SPELLS: Spells marked with an offensive symbol require a Magic Check, and the target's Magic Defense score determines the Difficulty Level. If the spell has multiple targets, it affects each target whose Magic Defense is equal to or lower than the total Result of your Magic Check (similarly to a multi attack). A critical success automatically hits all targets.

CALCULATING DAMAGE: Several spells calculate the potency of their effects using your High Roll (HR) - the highest die you rolled on your Magic Check. The same effect is applied to each target.

ENDING EFFECTS: A spell with a duration of Scene can be ended at any time. The effect also ends if you die, fall unconscious, or leave the scene.

CUMULATIVE EFFECTS: If the same spell is cast more than once on a target, its effects do not stack; the most recent casting replaces the previous one. If two spells have identical effects but different names, they do not stack.
rules.rituals_text:
By acquiring specific Classes and Skills, characters may gain the ability to use Ritual magic. Ritual disciplines and Magic Checks: Arcanism (WLP + WLP); Chimerism (INS + WLP or MIG + WLP); Elementalism (INS + WLP); Entropism (INS + WLP); Ritualism (INS + WLP); Spiritism (INS + WLP).

Ritual magic should never: cause direct damage (collateral damage is allowed); inflict or remove status effects; cause characters to lose or gain HP, MP, IP, Fabula Points or Ultima Points; replicate the mechanical benefits of an existing spell or Skill (narrative effects such as teleportation or flight are okay); or generate creatures or equipment, or grant them permanent abilities.

PROCESS: (1) Describe what you want to accomplish and declare the area or creatures affected. The GM has final say on whether an effect can be accomplished and which discipline it falls within. (2) The GM determines the total cost in Mind Points by consulting the area and potency tables. (3) The Ritual is cast following the normal procedure for a spell, but always requires a Magic Check meeting or exceeding a Difficulty Level based on the Ritual's potency, using the Attributes indicated by the discipline. On success the Ritual has the desired effect; on failure the GM describes how its effects have been twisted catastrophically.

POTENCY: Minor (20 MP, DL 7); Medium (30 MP, DL 10); Major (40 MP, DL 13); Extreme (50 MP, DL 16). AREA MULTIPLIER: Individual (x1); Small (x2); Large (x3); Huge (x4). Total MP cost = potency MP x area multiplier.

RITUALS AS GROUP CHECKS: Other characters can help (even without Ritual ability); the Magic Check becomes a Group Check with the performing character as leader.

REDUCING COST: Providing an especially rare or powerful ingredient cuts the MP cost in half (once per Ritual).

RITUALS DURING CONFLICTS: Use the Objective action to begin the Ritual (determine discipline, area, potency). A Ritual Clock is created with sections based on potency: Minor 4, Medium 6, Major 6, Extreme 8. Anyone can interact with the Clock via the Objective action. Once the Clock is filled, the initiator spends the appropriate Mind Points and performs the Magic Check (DL by potency) to resolve the Ritual.

entries.name: Elemental Shroud
entries.text: You weave magical energy and protect the targets from the fury of the elements. Choose a damage type: air, bolt, earth, fire or ice. Until this spell ends, each target gains Resistance against the chosen damage type.

entries.name: Elemental Weapon
entries.text: You imbue a weapon with elemental energy. Choose a damage type: air, bolt, earth, fire, or ice. Until this spell ends, all damage dealt by the weapon becomes of the chosen damage type. If you have that weapon equipped while you cast this spell, you may perform a free attack with it as part of the same action. This spell can only be cast on a weapon equipped by a willing creature.

entries.name: Flare
entries.text: You channel a single ray of fire towards your foe, its temperature so high that it will pierce through most defenses. The target suffers 【HR + 25】 fire damage. Damage dealt by this spell ignores Resistances.

entries.name: Fulgur
entries.opportunity: Each target hit by this spell suffers dazed.
entries.text: You weave electricity into a wave of crackling bolts. Each target hit by this spell suffers 【HR + 15】 bolt damage. Opportunity: Each target hit by this spell suffers dazed.

entries.name: Glacies
entries.opportunity: Each target hit by this spell suffers slow.
entries.text: You coat your foes under a thick layer of frost. Each target hit by this spell suffers 【HR + 15】 ice damage. Opportunity: Each target hit by this spell suffers slow.

entries.name: Iceberg
entries.text: A pillar of ice magic envelops your foe, suddenly dropping their body temperature to a critical level. The target suffers 【HR + 25】 ice damage. Damage dealt by this spell ignores Resistances.

entries.name: Ignis
entries.opportunity: Each target hit by this spell suffers shaken.
entries.text: You unleash a searing barrage against your foes, conjuring flames out of thin air. Each target hit by this spell suffers 【HR + 15】 fire damage. Opportunity: Each target hit by this spell suffers shaken.

entries.name: Soaring Strike
entries.text: The wind carries your strikes across the battlefield. You may immediately perform a free attack with a melee weapon you have equipped. This attack may target creatures that can only be targeted by ranged attacks. If you used a weapon belonging to the brawling or spear Category for this attack, it deals 5 extra damage. If you hit a flying target with this attack, you may force them to land immediately.

entries.name: Terra
entries.opportunity: Each target hit by this spell performs one fewer action on their next turn (to a minimum of 0 actions).
entries.text: Spires of jagged rock erupt from the ground beneath your foes, closing around them. Each target hit by this spell suffers 【HR + 15】 earth damage. This spell cannot target creatures who are flying, floating, falling, or otherwise in mid-air. Opportunity: Each target hit by this spell performs one fewer action on their next turn (to a minimum of 0 actions).

entries.name: Thunderbolt
entries.text: You send lightning striking at your foe. The target suffers 【HR + 25】 bolt damage. Damage dealt by this spell ignores Resistances.

entries.name: Ventus
entries.opportunity: Each flying target hit by this spell is forced to land immediately.
entries.text: You summon the power of winds against your enemy. Each target hit by this spell suffers 【HR + 15】 air damage. Opportunity: Each flying target hit by this spell is forced to land immediately.

entries.name: Vortex
entries.text: A roaring gale surrounds you, blowing away arrows and bullets. Until this spell ends, you gain a +2 bonus to your Defense against ranged attacks.

entries.name: Acceleration
entries.text: You bend the fabric of time. Until this spell ends, the target gains the ability to perform a single additional action during each of their turns. Once the target has performed a total of two additional actions granted by this spell, this spell ends.

entries.name: Anomaly
entries.text: You alter the very nature of your target. Until this spell ends, if the target would suffer damage of a type they Absorb or are Immune to, they are instead treated as if they were Vulnerable to that damage type. Once that happens, this spell ends.

entries.name: Dark Weapon
entries.text: You imbue a weapon with dark energy. Until this spell ends, all damage dealt by the weapon becomes of the dark type. If you have that weapon equipped while you cast this spell, you may perform a free attack with it as part of the same action. This spell can only be cast on a weapon equipped by a willing creature.

entries.name: Dispel
entries.text: You release a wave of negative energy and cleanse all magic from a creature. If the target is affected by one or more spells with a duration of Scene, they are no longer affected by any of those spells instead.

entries.name: Divination
entries.text: You glimpse briefly into the future. Until this spell ends, after a creature you can see performs a Check, if it was not a fumble nor a critical success, you may force that creature to reroll both dice. Once you have forced two rerolls this way, this spell ends.

entries.name: Drain Spirit
entries.text: You consume a creature's psyche. The target loses 【HR + 15】 Mind Points. Then, you recover an amount of Mind Points equal to half the Mind Points loss they suffered (if the loss was reduced to 0 in some way, you recover none).

entries.name: Drain Vigor
entries.text: You steal another creature's life force. The target suffers 【HR + 15】 dark damage. Then, you recover an amount of Hit Points equal to half the Hit Points loss they suffered (if the loss was reduced to 0 in some way, you recover none).

entries.name: Gamble
entries.text: You summon a vortex of chaotic energy. Roll your current Willpower die once for every 10 Mind Points spent while casting this spell, then keep the single die you prefer: the number on that die determines the effects of this spell. 1: You lose half of your current Hit Points and half of your current Mind Points. 2-3: Each creature present on the scene, including yourself, suffers poisoned. 4-6: Each creature present on the scene, including yourself, suffers slow. 7-8: Choose up to three creatures you can see: each of them recovers 50 Hit Points and also recovers from all status effects. 9+: Choose any number of creatures you can see: each of them suffers 30 damage. The damage type is determined randomly by rolling a d6: 1. air 2. bolt 3. dark 4. earth 5. fire 6. poison.

entries.name: Mirror
entries.text: You twist the laws of magic. Until this spell ends, if an offensive spell is cast on the target, the creature who cast that offensive spell will be targeted in their stead (any other targets of the offensive spell will be targeted as normal). Once that happens, this spell ends.

entries.name: Omega
entries.text: You invoke doom on your foe, turning strength into frailty. The target loses an amount of Hit Points equal to 【20 + half the target's level】.

entries.name: Stop
entries.text: You trap a foe inside a circle of altered time and space. The target will perform one fewer action on their next turn (to a minimum of 0 actions).

entries.name: Umbra
entries.opportunity: Each target hit by this spell suffers weak.
entries.text: A storm of dark energy turns matter into ash. Each target hit by this spell suffers 【HR + 15】 dark damage. Opportunity: Each target hit by this spell suffers weak.

entries.name: Aura
entries.text: You project your soul outside your body and direct it to surround the targets, shielding them from dangerous magic. Until this spell ends, each target may treat their Magic Defense as being equal to 12 against any effects that target it (they are still free to use their normal Defense score if higher than 12).

entries.name: Awaken
entries.text: You allow a creature to focus their vital energy into accomplishing what they previously could not. Choose one Attribute: Dexterity, Insight, Might, or Willpower. Until this spell ends, the target treats the chosen Attribute as if it were one die size higher (up to a maximum of d12).

entries.name: Barrier
entries.text: You project your soul outside your body and weave it into a barrier to protect the targets from attacks. Until this spell ends, each target may treat their Defense as being equal to 12 against any effects that target it (they are still free to use their normal Defense score if higher than 12).

entries.name: Cleanse
entries.text: You strengthen and purify the soul energy coursing through your companions. Each target recovers from all status effects.

entries.name: Enrage
entries.text: You cause a creature to lose any semblance of temper and act brazenly. The target suffers enraged and cannot perform the Guard or Spell actions during their next turn.

entries.name: Hallucination
entries.text: You alter the senses of your enemies, causing them to experience bizarre or frightening hallucinations. Choose dazed or shaken: you inflict the chosen status effect on each target hit by this spell.

entries.name: Heal
entries.text: You invigorate your companions, soothing their pain and healing their fatigue. Each target recovers 40 Hit Points. This amount increases to 50 Hit Points if you are level 20 or higher, or to 60 Hit Points if you are level 40 or higher.

entries.name: Lux
entries.opportunity: Each target hit by this spell suffers dazed.
entries.text: You focus your inner energy into a barrage of blinding soul rays. Each target hit by this spell suffers 【HR + 15】 light damage. Opportunity: Each target hit by this spell suffers dazed.

entries.name: Mercy
entries.text: You strengthen the heart of a creature against suffering and despair. Until this spell ends, if the target would be reduced to 0 Hit Points, they are instead left standing with exactly 1 Hit Point. Once that happens, this spell ends.

entries.name: Reinforce
entries.text: You protect the targets from attacks that would corrupt their body and spirit. Choose dazed, enraged, poisoned, shaken, slow, or weak. Until this spell ends, each target becomes immune to the chosen status effect.

entries.name: Soul Weapon
entries.text: You imbue a weapon with the cleansing energy of your spirit. Until this spell ends, all damage dealt by the weapon becomes of the light type. If you have that weapon equipped while you cast this spell, you may perform a free attack with it as part of the same action. This spell can only be cast on a weapon equipped by a willing creature.

entries.name: Torpor
entries.text: You smother the soul energy coursing through the bodies of your foes, hindering their movements. Choose slow or weak: you inflict the chosen status effect on each target hit by this spell.

entries.name: Hope
entries.text: The ultimate Spiritist spell, learned via the Hope Skill (requires mastery of the Spiritist Class). You rekindle the flames of courage and hope within a defeated hero. Choose a Player Character who surrendered but is still present on the scene: that Player Character immediately regains consciousness and recovers an amount of Hit Points equal to their Crisis score. Note that this spell will not undo the consequences of the character's surrender and is of no use if the character left the scene as part of their surrender - such as if they were captured and dragged away, or if they were trapped in a parallel dimension, and so on. A Player Character may only be affected by this spell once per scene; if a character is brought back into the conflict by this spell and is subsequently reduced to 0 Hit Points again, they must sacrifice themselves or surrender as per the normal rules (including a new consequence and 2 more Fabula Points if they surrender).

entries.name: Arctic Narcissus
entries.text: Magiseed (Chloromancy). It stubbornly survives in frozen territories. Growth Clock (T): [0-1] You are Resistant to earth and ice damage. [2-3] You and your allies present on the scene are Resistant to earth and ice damage.

entries.name: Blazing Chrysanthemum
entries.text: Magiseed (Chloromancy). Its thick petals resemble an erupting volcano. Growth Clock (T): [1-3] At the end of your turn, choose earth or fire. Until you use this effect again or this magiseed is no longer in your garden, all damage dealt by you and every ally present on the scene becomes the chosen type, its type cannot change, and it ignores Resistances.

entries.name: Desert Dahlia
entries.text: Magiseed (Chloromancy). A tenacious plant, it doesn't fear the dryness of dunes. Growth Clock (T): [0-1] You are Resistant to air and fire damage. [2-3] You and your allies present on the scene are Resistant to air and fire damage.

entries.name: Golden Ginkgo
entries.text: Magiseed (Chloromancy). Its vibrantly colored leaves represent tenacity. Growth Clock (T): [1] At the end of your turn, you and every ally you can see recover from dazed, enraged, and shaken. [2-3] At the end of your turn, you and every ally you can see recover an amount of Mind Points equal to 【5 + your Skill Level in Chloromancy】.

entries.name: Grave Asphodel
entries.text: Magiseed (Chloromancy). Solemn and elegant, it embodies death's ineluctability. Growth Clock (T): [1] At the end of your turn, you may choose an enemy you can see. If you do, that enemy suffers shaken. [2] At the end of your turn, every enemy you can see suffers shaken. [3] At the end of your turn, you deal an amount of dark damage equal to 【15 + 3 × your Skill Level in Chloromancy】 to each enemy you can see who is suffering from shaken.

entries.name: Hermit Iris
entries.text: Magiseed (Chloromancy). Its leaves carry whispers of ancient knowledge. Growth Clock (T): [1] At the end of your turn, choose an enemy you can see. The Game Master reveals its Level, Rank, Species, maximum Hit Points, maximum Mind Points, Traits, Attributes, Defense, and Magic Defense. [2-3] The Game Master also reveals its Affinities.

entries.name: Hookleaf Nightshade
entries.text: Magiseed (Chloromancy). Its purplish berries contain a deadly magical venom. Growth Clock (T): [0-1] All damage you deal becomes poison, its type cannot change, and it ignores Immunities and Resistances. [2-3] All damage you deal becomes poison, its type cannot change, and it ignores Immunities and Resistances. Additionally, when you deal poison damage for the first time during your turn, you deal extra damage equal to 【your Skill Level in Chloromancy】 and each creature that lost Hit Points this way suffers poisoned.

entries.name: Horned Hawthorn
entries.text: Magiseed (Chloromancy). Favored by witches and surgeons alike for its purifying properties. Growth Clock (T): [0-1] You are Resistant to dark and poison damage. [2-3] You and your allies present on the scene are Resistant to dark and poison damage.

entries.name: Lunar Magnolia
entries.text: Magiseed (Chloromancy). It blooms in the darkest hours of the night. Growth Clock (T): [1-3] At the end of your turn, choose ice or light. Until you use this effect again or this magiseed is no longer in your garden, all damage dealt by you and every ally present on the scene becomes the chosen type, its type cannot change, and it ignores Resistances.

entries.name: Ocean Lotus
entries.text: Magiseed (Chloromancy). A graceful symbol of serenity and wisdom. Growth Clock (T): [0-1] You are immune to dazed and slow. [2] You treat your Dexterity and Insight as being one die size higher (up to a maximum of d12). [3] You and every ally you can see treat your Dexterity and Insight as being one die size higher (up to a maximum of d12).

entries.name: Pilgrim Gazalia
entries.text: Magiseed (Chloromancy). Its flamboyant corolla is synonymous with abundance. Growth Clock (T): [1-2] At the end of your turn, choose yourself or another Player Character you can see. The chosen character regains 2 Inventory Points. [3] At the end of your turn, you and every other Player Character you can see regain 1 Inventory Point.

entries.name: Prancing Dandelion
entries.text: Magiseed (Chloromancy). A symbol of energy and vivacity, it spreads seeds through stormy winds. Growth Clock (T): [1-3] At the end of your turn, choose air or bolt. Until you use this effect again or this magiseed is no longer in your garden, all damage dealt by you and every ally present on the scene becomes the chosen type, its type cannot change, and it ignores Resistances.

entries.name: Regal Protea
entries.text: Magiseed (Chloromancy). A colorful and tenacious flower, favored by an ancient dynasty. Growth Clock (T): [0-1] You are immune to shaken and weak. [2] You treat your Might and Willpower as being one die size higher (up to a maximum of d12). [3] You and every ally you can see treat your Might and Willpower as being one die size higher (up to a maximum of d12).

entries.name: Remedy Lily
entries.text: Magiseed (Chloromancy). Its elegant flowers filter and purify spiritual energy. Growth Clock (T): [1] At the end of your turn, you and every ally you can see recover from poisoned, slow, and weak. [2-3] At the end of your turn, you and every ally you can see recover an amount of Hit Points equal to 【15 + your Skill Level in Chloromancy】.

entries.name: Serrated Rose
entries.text: Magiseed (Chloromancy). Its thorns form a tangle equally breathtaking and dangerous. Growth Clock (T): [1] At the end of your turn, you may choose an enemy you can see. If you do, that enemy suffers slow. [2] At the end of your turn, every enemy you can see suffers slow. [3] At the end of your turn, you deal an amount of physical damage equal to 【15 + 3 × your Skill Level in Chloromancy】 to each enemy you can see who is suffering from slow.

entries.name: Silver Strelitzia
entries.text: Magiseed (Chloromancy). Its silvery blossoms are easily mistaken for jewels. Growth Clock (T): [0-1] You are Resistant to bolt and light damage. [2-3] You and your allies present on the scene are Resistant to bolt and light damage.

entries.name: Star Peony
entries.text: Magiseed (Chloromancy). It silently gazes into celestial depths. Growth Clock (T): [1] At the end of your turn, you may choose an enemy you can see. If you do, that enemy suffers dazed. [2] At the end of your turn, every enemy you can see suffers dazed. [3] At the end of your turn, you deal an amount of light damage equal to 【15 + 3 × your Skill Level in Chloromancy】 to each enemy you can see who is suffering from dazed.

entries.name: Striped Orchid
entries.text: Magiseed (Chloromancy). Each segment of this plant is covered in toxins. Growth Clock (T): [1] At the end of your turn, you may choose an enemy you can see. If you do, that enemy suffers weak. [2] At the end of your turn, every enemy you can see suffers weak. [3] At the end of your turn, you deal an amount of poison damage equal to 【15 + 3 × your Skill Level in Chloromancy】 to each enemy you can see who is suffering from weak.

entries.name: Wardwattle
entries.text: Magiseed (Chloromancy). A tangle of robust branches, whose thorns spread powerful poison. Growth Clock (T): [0-1] You are Resistant to physical damage. [2] You are Resistant to physical damage. Additionally, after a creature hits you with a melee attack, you deal an amount of physical damage to that creature equal to 【5 + your Skill Level in Chloromancy】 (after their attack was resolved). [3] You are Resistant to physical damage. Additionally, after a creature hits you with a melee attack, you deal an amount of physical damage to that creature equal to 【5 + your Skill Level in Chloromancy】, and then deal an amount of poison damage to that creature equal to 【5 + your Skill Level in Chloromancy】 (both these damage instances happen after that creature's attack was resolved).

entries.name: Wrathful Carnation
entries.text: Magiseed (Chloromancy). Despite their beauty, its yellow flowers emit an irritatingly pungent smell. Growth Clock (T): [1-4] When this magiseed leaves your garden, choose up to one enemy you can see. Until the end of your next turn, when the chosen enemy performs an attack or casts an offensive spell (r), they must include you among the targets of that attack or spell (if able). At the end of your turn, you may remove this magiseed from your garden; if you do, erase all sections of your Growth Clock.

entries.name: Aero Blast
entries.text: Air wellspring invocation ("blast"). You deal 20 air damage to the target. If an invocation deals damage, it deals 5 extra damage if you are level 20 or higher, or 10 extra if level 40 or higher.

entries.name: Aero Hex
entries.duration: Until the start of your next turn
entries.text: Air wellspring invocation ("hex"). Until the start of your next turn, the target suffers 5 extra damage from all sources that deal bolt and fire damage.

entries.name: Breeze
entries.text: Air wellspring invocation. The target recovers 30 Hit Points. If an invocation causes Hit Point recovery, the amount increases by 10 if you are level 20 or higher, or by 20 if level 40 or higher.

entries.name: Twister
entries.text: Air wellspring invocation. You deal 10 air damage to the target, and they suffer dazed.

entries.name: Geo Blast
entries.text: Earth wellspring invocation ("blast"). You deal 20 earth damage to the target.

entries.name: Geo Hex
entries.duration: Until the start of your next turn
entries.text: Earth wellspring invocation ("hex"). Until the start of your next turn, the target suffers 5 extra damage from all sources that deal air and ice damage.

entries.name: Growth
entries.text: Earth wellspring invocation. The target recovers 30 Hit Points.

entries.name: Quicksand
entries.text: Earth wellspring invocation. You deal 10 earth damage to the target, and they suffer slow.

entries.name: Pyro Blast
entries.text: Fire wellspring invocation ("blast"). You deal 20 fire damage to the target.

entries.name: Pyro Hex
entries.duration: Until the start of your next turn
entries.text: Fire wellspring invocation ("hex"). Until the start of your next turn, the target suffers 5 extra damage from all sources that deal earth and ice damage.

entries.name: Burst
entries.text: Fire wellspring invocation. You deal 10 fire damage to the target, and they suffer shaken.

entries.name: Smoke
entries.text: Fire wellspring invocation. The target loses 20 Mind Points and also suffers weak.

entries.name: Electro Blast
entries.text: Lightning wellspring invocation ("blast"). You deal 20 bolt damage to the target.

entries.name: Electro Hex
entries.duration: Until the start of your next turn
entries.text: Lightning wellspring invocation ("hex"). Until the start of your next turn, the target suffers 5 extra damage from all sources that deal earth and fire damage.

entries.name: Static
entries.text: Lightning wellspring invocation. You deal 10 bolt damage to the target, and they suffer slow.

entries.name: Thunder
entries.text: Lightning wellspring invocation. The target loses 20 Mind Points and also suffers dazed.

entries.name: Hydro Blast
entries.text: Water wellspring invocation ("blast"). You deal 20 ice damage to the target.

entries.name: Hydro Hex
entries.duration: Until the start of your next turn
entries.text: Water wellspring invocation ("hex"). Until the start of your next turn, the target suffers 5 extra damage from all sources that deal air and bolt damage.

entries.name: Chill
entries.text: Water wellspring invocation. The target loses 20 Mind Points and also suffers shaken.

entries.name: Frostbite
entries.text: Water wellspring invocation. You deal 10 ice damage to the target, and they suffer weak.
